title:
Lesson 1:
Sure / Of Course / With Pleasure
description: 'Claro' = sure / of course. 'Por supuesto' = of course (stronger). 'Con gusto' = with pleasure. All three express willingness and agreement, from casual to formal.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Can you help me with this?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Are you sure? I don't want to bother you.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Por supuesto. No es ninguna molestia.
dialogue.english: Of course. It's no bother at all.

title:
Lesson 2:
Please / Excuse Me / I'm Sorry
description: 'Por favor' = please. 'Disculpe' = excuse me (getting attention, formal). 'Lo siento' = I'm sorry (expressing regret). Each has a different use — learning when to use which is key.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Excuse me, where is the bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Lo siento, no sé. No soy de aquí.
dialogue.english: I'm sorry, I don't know. I'm not from here.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: No hay problema. Por favor, ¿alguien más sabe?
dialogue.english: No problem. Please, does anyone else know?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Lo siento mucho. Espero que lo encuentres.
dialogue.english: I'm very sorry. I hope you find it.

title:
Lesson 3:
It Doesn't Matter
description: 'No importa' = It doesn't matter / Never mind (casual, dismissing something). 'No es importante' = It's not important (more explanatory). Use 'no importa' to wave something off quickly.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I arrived late. I'm very sorry.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No importa. Todavía estamos esperando al resto.
dialogue.english: It doesn't matter. We're still waiting for the others.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I also forgot to bring the documents.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: No es importante para hoy. Los traes la próxima vez.
dialogue.english: It's not important for today. Bring them next time.
